fields.description:
## Context

The 2026-07-08 principal-architect audit (security / reliability / maintainability / OSS best practices; four deep-dive reviews plus bandit, semgrep, pip-audit, gitleaks, zizmor, radon) found no critical security issues but a cluster of high-leverage reliability seams and hygiene gaps. Reconciler-internal findings were routed to epic adept-hedge-stain (including the dead-safety-machinery story nonmystic-archetypic-gibbon); everything else lands here. Each child cites the audit finding it remediates.

Reliability deliverables in one place (so the AC terms below are defined):
- **claim-race surfacing** (child 3003): a lost cross-clone claim becomes observable to the loser (retroactive signal once the merge is visible) and via fsck/validate/show.
- **push-merge lock** (child e699): the push-retry fetch+merge runs under the store write lock so it can't race a concurrent locked write.
- **staged-event reset on write failure** (child 0e59): the event-append failure path resets the git index (`git reset -- <relpath>`), not just the worktree file, so a failed write's staged blob can't be swept into the next successful commit; the git-add return code is also checked before commit.
- **LLM timeout wiring** (child 02cd): the operator's REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT / cfg.timeout_s actually bounds LLM calls.
- **unmapped-status guard** (child 5886): an inbound Jira status with no mapping is skipped + alerted instead of silently defaulting to open.

## Success Criteria (outcomes, not mechanisms)

- A cross-clone claim that loses the merge race is surfaced to the losing claimant and pinned by a two-clone regression test
- No git-store write path can commit another write's leftovers, and the push-retry merge cannot race a concurrent locked write
- The operator-configured LLM timeout demonstrably bounds LLM calls, and is never silently lowered below the SDK default when unset. NOTE: the exact pydantic-ai API surface that receives the timeout (model HTTP-client settings vs. model_settings vs. a run_sync kwarg vs. an asyncio wrapper) is a child-02cd implementation decision — 02cd selects the surface for the installed pydantic-ai version, proves it with a test that asserts the value reaches the client, and records the choice; the epic asserts only the observable outcome.
- Inbound Jira statuses that have no mapping can no longer silently reopen local tickets (skipped + alerted + tallied)
- Typing and config-surface ratchets are in place (mypy strictness ratchet started; env-var registry with a CI drift gate)
- Docs drift called out by the audit is fixed; CodeQL runs on the mirror; docs are navigable by humans; repo hygiene items closed

The audit's "fail closed on unreadable LLM config" idea is NOT covered by any child in this graph; it is parked as idea sluggish-moistful-chital (linked discovered_from) because it is a behaviour inversion needing its own design/rollback decision, out of scope for this stacked remediation.

## Sequencing & rollout note

Children land as a single ordered Gerrit stack (bottom→top), so files touched by multiple children compose in commit order rather than conflicting: docs/concurrency.md (3e8b then 3003), README.md (09d8 badges, 3775 index link, d13e supported-versions), pyproject.toml (3775 urls, d13e classifier, d2fa mypy), test.yml (0f21 drift gate, d13e 3.13 matrix).

Rollout/rollback: every runtime change here is additive or hardens an existing best-effort path (no new user-facing surface, no data migration), each is an independently-gated Gerrit change, and rollback is a per-change `git revert` — no feature flag is warranted for this scope. CodeQL (c3f8) is intrinsically post-landing: its first mirror run only executes after the workflow file reaches main, so first-run finding triage is tracked as a post-landing follow-up, not a pre-merge close blocker.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All child stories/tasks closed with green CI (Gerrit Verified +1 per change)
- [ ] Reliability children landed with regression tests: claim-race surfacing (3003), push-merge lock (e699), staged-event reset on write failure (0e59), LLM timeout wiring (02cd), unmapped-status guard (5886)
- [ ] Maintainability children (mypy ratchet start d2fa, env-var registry + drift gate 0f21) landed
- [ ] Docs children (stale shell-test citations + architecture remedy row 3e8b, docs index + user guide 3775) landed
- [ ] OSS/CI children (CodeQL workflow c3f8 — file valid & landed, first-run triage tracked post-landing; README badges 09d8; Python 3.13 matrix d13e; .joe-janitor/.tokensave hygiene 2440) landed
